macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Mixed Amid Trump Iran Escalation Threats
items.summary: US President Trump's warnings of significant escalation in the Iran war shadowed stock markets, with stocks closing mixed after earlier gains; oil prices soared over 10% on Thursday due to energy shocks. Dow closed up 1,000 points on March 31 as Trump signaled possible de-escalation.[2][3]

items.title: OPEC+ Plans Symbolic May Oil Quota Increase Despite Middle East Conflict
items.summary: OPEC+ members intend to raise production quotas for May in a symbolic gesture, as war in the Middle East limits output and shipments from key producers. This comes amid rising global energy prices and supply disruptions.[2]

items.title: Gas Prices Surge Past $4/Gallon Amid Iran War
items.summary: US gas prices exceeded $4 per gallon for the first time since 2022, driven by the Iran conflict, with oil prices up and surcharges impacting shipping costs like Amazon's 3.5% fee on sellers. Expectations are for continued rises.[3]

items.title: Indian Rupee Rallies on RBI Intervention
items.summary: The Indian rupee extended its biggest surge in 12 years after the Reserve Bank of India doubled down on curbing speculation, potentially spurring demand for gold and property. India also removing 2,000 rupee notes.[2]

items.title: Upcoming US Inflation Data in Focus
items.summary: Key US inflation reports, including March CPI and February PCE, are set for release this week, alongside Middle East developments; WSJ poll projects CPI at 1.3% YoY. Markets await amid global tensions.[1]

items.title: Trump Imposes 100% Tariffs on Some Pharmaceuticals
items.summary: US President Trump signed an executive order imposing 100% tariffs on certain pharmaceuticals, escalating trade measures amid broader geopolitical tensions.[3]

items.title: Barito Renewables Shares Hit Two-Year Low on Ownership Concerns
items.summary: Indonesian firm PT Barito Renewables Energy's shares fell to a two-year low after the stock exchange flagged its highly concentrated shareholder base.[2]

items.title: South Korea BOK Expected to Hold Rates at 2.50%
items.summary: South Korea's central bank is consensus-expected to maintain its policy rate at 2.50% amid Middle East uncertainty, inflation risks, and leadership transition.[1]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges to $69,500 Amid $250M+ Liquidations of Short Positions
items.summary: Bitcoin rallied to $69,500 with trading volume up 62%, triggering over $250 million in liquidations, mostly bearish shorts, as the global crypto market cap rose nearly 2% to $2.36-$2.44 trillion. Ethereum hit $2,130, XRP and Dogecoin also gained amid heightened market activity.

items.title: Crypto Rally Fueled by Trump's Mixed Signals on Iran Conflict
items.summary: Cryptocurrencies rebounded despite Trump's threats to Iran's infrastructure and Strait of Hormuz deadline, with mixed hardline and negotiation tones boosting sentiment; Bitcoin up 2.8% to $69,200 and Ether up 3.7% in early Asia trading.

items.title: Drift Protocol Suffers $285M Loss in Social Engineering Attack
items.summary: Drift Protocol faced a major security breach via social engineering, resulting in $285 million losses due to vulnerabilities, amid rising AI-driven threats to crypto security highlighted by Ledger's Charles Guillemet.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Exceed $209M in 24 Hours, Bitcoin Dominant
items.summary: Perpetual futures liquidations topped $209 million, with Bitcoin accounting for the largest share, reflecting volatile trading tied to broader market rebounds and geopolitical tensions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Source Code Leak Reveals Claude's Memory Architecture and Kairos System
items.summary: A publishing error on March 31st exposed Claude Code's source maps, revealing Anthropic's undisclosed autonomous mode called 'Kairos,' a three-layer memory architecture with automatic dream-like memory consolidation, and anti-distillation mechanisms designed to prevent competitor model copying.

items.title: Anthropic Achieves Unprecedented Growth: $1B to $19B ARR in 14 Months
items.summary: Anthropic has experienced the fastest growth trajectory in AI history, scaling from $1 billion to over $19 billion in annual recurring revenue in just 14 months, driven by Claude's rapid adoption and the company's focus on AI coding capabilities.

items.title: Andrej Karpathy Develops No-Code AI Memory System Using LLMs
items.summary: Karpathy has created an AI-powered 'second brain' system that uses large language models to convert messy notes into a clean, linked wiki, reducing coding time while enabling knowledge queries through an AI interface.

items.title: Claude Code Introduces Advanced Planning Features
items.summary: Anthropic has released improved planning capabilities in Claude Code, with infrastructure for testing unreleased models to measure user acceptance and inform model quality improvements.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Street Fighter 6 Reveals Ingrid Trailer, Sparking Speculation on Future Changes
items.summary: Capcom released a trailer for Ingrid in Street Fighter 6, generating buzz due to her unusual space god character and potential links to a canceled game, Capcom Fighting Allstars. Fans speculate this could lead to system mechanic changes, guest characters, and collaborations like Capcom vs. SNK 3.

items.title: 'Star Citizen' Nears Major Milestone After 14 Years in Development
items.summary: Star Citizen is approaching a significant milestone with its single-player campaign Squadron 42, featuring stars like Mark Hamill, Gillian Anderson, and Henry Cavill, backed by over a billion dollars in funding.

items.title: Evercade Announces Nexus 64 Themed Version and Multiplayer Features
items.summary: Evercade revealed the Nexus 64, an N64-colored version of the Nexus handheld, with new multiplayer options allowing one cartridge across multiple systems without internet.

items.title: LEGO Fortnite x NINJAGO Adds Katana as New Best Weapon
items.summary: The LEGO Fortnite Odyssey x NINJAGO update introduces the katana, hailed as the strongest weapon yet, with new stun tricks, glitches, secondary attacks, and katana builds.

items.title: Roblox Rivals Update News: Bugs, Canceled Content, and Easter Absence
items.summary: Roblox Rivals features updates on game-breaking bugs like Daisy’s extra bug nets, a canceled update, no Easter event, fake games, and secrets on the Graveyard map.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Microsoft Forces Windows 11 24H2 to 25H2 Update with No Opt-Out
items.summary: Microsoft is automatically updating Windows 11 24H2 devices to version 25H2 using an ML-based system, with no full opt-out option ahead of 24H2's end-of-support on October 13.

items.title: Wipro Acquires Mindsprint for $375M and Signs $1B Contract with Olam
items.summary: Indian IT giant Wipro has agreed to buy Singapore-based Olam's IT services arm Mindsprint for $375 million and secured an eight-year, $1 billion contract with Olam.
